name: Mystery Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Dry Yeast OR 2 Pre-Measured Packages
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (5O°C).
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine yeast, milk, aniseed and 2 cups unbleached flour.
3. In a saucepan, combine juices, water, orange rind, cinnamon, honey and 1 tbsp oil. Heat until warm (105 to 115°F). Pour over dry ingredients. Beat with a wooden spoon for 1 minute. Cover with plastic wrap and let stand for 10 minutes.
4. Add whole-wheat flour, buckwheat flour and all but 1/2 cup of the remaining unbleached flour, adding it in 1/2 cup increments, beating with a wooden spoon after each addition. After adding 4 1/2 cups of flour, the dough will become difficult to beat with a wooden spoon. Scoop up and turn onto lightly floured board and knead, adding more flour as needed to make a smooth and elastic dough.
5. Shape dough into a ball. Drop into a lightly oiled, fairly straight-sided bowl, turning to coat. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and let rise at room temperature (70 to 80°F) until doubled in bulk (about 1 hour 15 minutes).
6. Punch down dough. Transfer to board and cut into 3 equal parts. Cover with waxed paper and let dough rest for 5 minutes.
7. Roll each piece out into a 7 x 12 inch rectangle, starting with the short end. Roll up tightly, tucking in sides and pressing seams to hold. Place seam side down in 3 small loaf pans (7 1/8 x 3 5/8 x 2 1/4 inches). Grease with margarine or cooking spray. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise at room temperature until doubled in bulk (about 1 hour).
8. Gently remove plastic wrap. Bake in preheated oven for 40 minutes. Remove bread from pans and place back directly on the rack, baking for an additional 5 minutes. Loaves should be crispy and golden brown. Let cool thoroughly before slicing.
